metadata.trigger: User feedback: Sucrose is a key player and needed deeper coverage

summary: Iter 4 is a targeted Sucrose-enrichment pass, not a structural rebuild. 5 Sucrose edges added, network grows from 69 to 74 edges. Sucrose now has in=1/out=8 (true hub status reflecting its role as a primary sugar-signaling integrator). MAX2 loses source status (gains Sucrose inhibition - the Perception Gate becomes sucrose-modulated, which is biologically accurate per Patil 2022 finding that sucrose antagonizes SL signaling at multiple points including MAX2 transcript repression). Phenotype hits the 5-activator cap (CK, PIN1, PIN3, T6P, Sucrose) - at max but within §10 quality criteria. Two deliberate exclusions documented (Sucrose->D14, Sucrose->Auxin both predict wrong direction in current framework).

status_changes.Sucrose: source -> downstream (gained Decapitation upstream; now has 8 outgoing edges reflecting hub role)

pathway_completeness.justification: Sucrose pathway now adequately deep: direct branching promotion (E288), CK biosynthesis promotion (E280), SL antagonism via MAX2 repression (E286), PIN1 upregulation (E287), plus the existing Sucrose -| Strigolactone, Sucrose -| BRC1, Sucrose -> T6P, Sucrose -| HB21 paths. 8 outgoing edges from a single node make it a proper hub rather than an isolated input. Now aligns with the user's 'basic player' biology framing.

mechanistic_depth.justification: Sucrose enrichment improves depth. Decapitation now has 5 outgoing paths (Auxin-, SL-, IPT, Sucrose, BRC1) reflecting the multi-mechanism nature of apical dominance release. Perception Gate remains intact (SL has only SL->D14 outgoing) but is now modulatable via Sucrose -| MAX2.

motif_coverage.justification: Unchanged - all 6 motifs still active. Sucrose additions fit Motif 3 (Coherent Feed-Forward): Sucrose promotes branching via 4 independent paths (direct, CK, PIN1, MAX2 suppression) - a proper redundant signaling hub.

cascade_balance.justification: Source % dropped to 21.1% - further below the 30-50% target band. Phenotype now at 5/5 activators (at hard max per §10). PIN1 at 5 activators. Cytokinin now 2 act + 1 inh. Balance is still within hard caps (7/direction, 5 phenotype activators) but the network is denser on the downstream side than the target band prefers. Score -1 reflects the drift from target, not a violation.

hub_completeness.justification: Sucrose now properly represented as a central hub (8 outgoing edges - the 2nd-highest outdegree in the network after Auxin's 7). BRC1 hub unchanged at 10 inputs.

topology_hazards.justification: Unchanged. No new positive feedback loops introduced - all 5 new Sucrose edges are coherent-direction (sucrose promotes branching net effect preserved across all paths). Sucrose -> D14 and Sucrose -> Auxin explicitly skipped to avoid wrong-direction predictions (documented in build_log).

evidence_quality.justification: Unchanged. All 5 new edges have HIGH-confidence primary literature evidence (Patil 2022 Mol Plant, Bertheloot 2020 New Phytologist, Fichtner 2022, etc.).

phenotype_audit.justification: Phenotype now at 5/5 activators (at cap). Metabolite class (T6P, Sucrose) now represented by 2 activators. Still balanced between 5 promoters and 2 inhibitors.

rejected_edges_review.justification: Coverage rose from 86/313 to 91/313. Sucrose -> D14 (E281) and Sucrose -> Auxin (E278) explicitly excluded with framework-mismatch rationale - transparent about the model limitation.

suggestions.description: Source % at 21.1% is materially below 30-50% target band
suggestions.biological_justification: Iter 4 made Sucrose and MAX2 downstream, reducing source count to 8/38. Further reduction would require restoring either node to source (biologically retrograde). To raise source %, Step 1 would need to find curated upstream regulators for environment-adjacent nodes (e.g., Phosphorus, Light, Temperature variants) OR drop some of the well-regulated downstream nodes (would reduce biology). Recommend accepting 21.1% as the price of deeper Sucrose/MAX2 biology.
suggestions.implementation: No action. Policy note: the 30-50% target may be too tight for networks with multiple hub-player nodes that all have curated upstream regulation.

suggestions.description: Framework limitation: Sucrose -> D14 and Sucrose -> Auxin excluded to avoid wrong-direction predictions
suggestions.biological_justification: Sucrose -> D14 (+1) is spatially complex: sucrose stabilizes INACTIVE D14 (SL-unbound), but the current framework treats all D14 levels as functional. Sucrose -> Auxin (+1) captures bud-local auxin biosynthesis but the global Auxin node would route through Auxin -| IPT, producing the opposite branching prediction from the net biology. These are framework limitations, not extraction gaps. The literature is correct; the model abstraction loses spatial/conformational resolution.
suggestions.implementation: Framework enhancement needed: either (a) separate bud-local vs apical Auxin nodes, (b) treat D14 as two-state (SL-bound active vs unbound inactive) - significant architectural change deferred.

literature_gaps: Persistent from iter 3: CYP707A3 -> ABA direct (ABA catabolism)
Persistent from iter 3: SMXL678 -> CKX9 ChIP-seq direct (would resolve Trap 5)
Persistent from iter 3: PIF5 upstream (Low_R_FR -> PIF5 not curated)
New iter 4 awareness: bud-local vs global hormone modeling required to include Sucrose -> Auxin and Sucrose -> D14 correctly
stop_reason: User request satisfied: Sucrose now properly represented as key player with 8 outgoing edges. No new HIGH/MEDIUM suggestions. Two LOW priority items document remaining tradeoffs. Per JUDGE_AGENT.md §11 stop conditions: approved.
iteration_notes: Final v47 build: 38 nodes, 74 edges, 8 sources (21.1%). Curated coverage: 91/313 (29.1%). Mean rubric: iter1=3.8, iter2=4.2, iter3=4.5, iter4=4.4 (slight drop from iter3 due to cascade_balance score -1). This iteration prioritized biological completeness over structural targets - a defensible user-directed trade-off.
